content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
March 15, 1989 
Honorable James V. Singley 
Councilmember 
22795 Barton Road 
Grand Terrace, California 92324-5295 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-083 
Dear Councilmember Singley: 
This is in response to your request for advice regarding 
application of the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act")l/ to the city's review and 
approval of a contract for law enforcement services. 
This 
letter "confirms the telephone advice I provided to you on 
March 14, 1989. 
Our advice is limited to laws included in the Political 
Reform Act. 
Please consult your city attorney or county 
counsel regarding other provisions of law that might apply 
and in particular as regards Government Code section 1090 
which prohibits a public official from having a financial 
interest in a contract. 
QUESTION 
May a councilmember who is also an employee of the 
sheriff's department participate in contract negotiations 
between the city and the sheriff's department? 
CONCLUSION 
Under the Act, a council member who is also an employee 
of the sheriff's department may participate in contract 
negotiations between the city and the sheriff's department. 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Honorable James V. singley 
March 15, 1989 
Page 2 
FACTS 
The city of Grand Terrace contracts with the San 
Bernardino County Sheriff's Department for law enforcement 
services. 
The city council must review and approve the 
sheriff's contract for law enforcement services on an annual 
basis. 
The city does not control the salaries of sheriff's 
employees. 
The contract only relates to the amount or level 
of law enforcement services provided. 
You are a councilmember for the city and are also 
employed with the sheriff's department as a sheriff's flight 
sergeant assigned to the sheriff's aviation division. 
Your 
position with the sheriff's aviation division is not affected 
by the contract for law enforcement services. 
ANALYSIS 
section 87100 prohibits a public official from making, 
participating in, or using his official position to influence 
any governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to 
know he has a financial interest. 
A member of the city 
council is a public official. 
(Section 82048; Regulation 
18700(a), copy enclosed.) 
An official has a financial interest in a decision if it 
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a 
material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on 
the pubic generally, on the official or any member of his or 
her immediate family or on: 
(0) 
Any source of income, other than gifts and 
other than loans by a commercial lending 
institution in the regular course of business on 
terms available to the public without regard to 
official status, aggregating two hundred and fifty 
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to, 
received by or promised to the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the 
decision is made. 
Section 87103(c). 
The effect of a decision is material if any person, 
including a business entity, which has been a source of 
income to the official of $250 or more in the preceding 12 
months is directly involved in a decision before the 
official's agency. 
(Regulation 18702.1(a), copy enclosed.) 


Honorable James V. singley 
March IS, 1989 
Page 3 
The term "income" is defined in section 82030. 
section 
82030 provides in pertinent part that income does not 
include: 
Salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem 
received from a state, local, or federal government 
agency ... 
(Section 82030 (b) (2) .) 
Pursuant to the above statutory provisions, any income 
you receive from the sheriff's department would not make the 
sheriff's department a potentially disqualifying source of 
income to you under section 87103(c), supra. 
Therefore, 
although during the contract negotiations the sheriff's 
department will be directly before the city council, your 
salary as a sheriff's flight sergeant is not grounds for 
disqualification under the Act. 
(In re Moore (1977) 3 FPPC 
Ops. 33; Darcy Advice Letter, No. I-87-296; Boomer Advice 
Letter, No. I-88-291, copies enclosed.) 
Should you have any questions or should you want to 
discuss the matter further, do not hesitate to call me at 
(916) 322-5901. 
DMG:BMB:aa 
Enclosures 
Very truly yours, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
~; ,', 
l. 
By: 
Blanca M. Breeze 
Counsel, Legal Division 


t*",~~~ 
February 2, 1989 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Gentlemen/Ladies: 
BYRON R. MATTESON 
Mayor 
BARBARA PFENNIG HAUSEN 
Mayor Pro Tem 
Council Members 
HUGH J< GRANT 
DENNIS L EVANS 
SUSAN CRAWFORD 
THOMAS J< SCHWAB 
City Manager 
I 
was recently appointed to fill a vacant City Council 
position 
with 
the City of Grand Terrace. 
I am requesting a ruling 
from 
your 
Commission 
as 
to what position I must 
take 
regarding 
a 
possible conflict of interest issue. 
I 
am employed as a Sheriff's Flight Sergeant and assigned to the 
Sheriff's Aviation Division. 
The City of Grand Terrace contracts 
with 
the 
San 
Bernardino County Sheriff's 
Department 
for 
law 
enforcement 
services 
and, 
the 
City Council 
must 
review 
and 
approve the Sheriff's contract for law enforcement services on an 
annual 
basis. 
The City has no say in what the salaries of 
any 
Sheriff's 
employee 
is, 
and the contract only 
deals 
with 
the 
amount or level of law enforcement services provided. 
In no way 
is 
my position with the Sheriff's Aviation Division affected 
by 
the 
contract for the law enforcement services from the Sheriff's 
Department for the City of Grand Terrace. 
I am requesting a ruling from the Commission as to whether I 
can 
participate in the upcoming contract negotiations with the 
City 
and 
the Sheriff's Department or whether I must abstain from this 
issue. 
Thank 
you for your consideration of this issue and 
your 
prompt 
response. 
Sincerely, 
Council 
JVS:bt 
CC: 
Thomas Schwab, City Manager 
John Harper, City Attorney 
ROAD .. GRAND TERRACE, CA 92324-5295 • CIVIC CENTER -
(714) 824-6621 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
James V. Singley 
Councilmernber 
city of Grand Terrace 
22795 Barton Road 
February 7, 1989 
Grand Terrace, CA 
92324-5295 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-083 
Dear Mr. Singley: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on February 6, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Blanca Breeze an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329.) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:ld 
Very truly yours, 
~~" ~-4U 
Diane M. Griffiths ~ 
General Counsel 
cc: 
Thomas Schwab, City Manager 
John Harper, city Attorney 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660
